Partner With Us

Ecosystem & Collaboration

Knowledge You Belong To.

RFTT is not just content — it is an ecosystem. A structured space of collaboration between entrepreneurs, universities, corporates, policy actors, and youth development organisations working toward a shared vision for African entrepreneurship.

Key Ecosystem Nodes

Our Strategic Partners

Y

YLED

Youth-Led Enterprise Development

A flagship initiative driving structured youth entrepreneurship across South Africa.

G

GIBS

Gordon Institute of Business Science

Academic partnership bringing rigour and institutional credibility to entrepreneurship education.

N

NWU

North-West University

Collaborative research and curriculum development for African-centred entrepreneurship.

T

TWIMS

Township Wisdom & Strategy

Bridging township-born enterprise knowledge with formal business strategy frameworks.

What We Do Together

Community Initiatives

01

Dialogue Forums

Structured conversations that bring together entrepreneurs, academics, corporates, and policy actors around pressing ecosystem challenges.

02

Youth Entrepreneurship Dialogue (YED)

A dedicated platform where young entrepreneurs engage with mentors, funders, and peers in facilitated strategic dialogue.

03

Book Donation Drives

Distributing entrepreneurship books, case studies, and workbooks to under-resourced schools and community libraries.

04

Educator Roundtables

Bringing educators together to share best practices, challenges, and innovations in teaching African entrepreneurship.

05

Entrepreneur Mentoring Circles

Peer-to-peer and expert-guided mentoring structures that sustain entrepreneurs beyond the inspiration stage.

Be Part of the Ecosystem

Whether you are an entrepreneur, educator, corporate partner, or policy maker — there is a place for you in the RFTT community.

Join our community and receive stories
that inspire purpose and progress.

Celebrating determination, resilience, and success. Building stronger communities through inspiration and opportunity.
